title: EnableDevTools (HTML Widget)
text: EnableDevTools (HTML Widget) EnableDevTools is an HTML widget property that allows a user to enable the DevTools (Developer Tools) of Chromium Embedded Framework (CEF). EnableDevTools = True/False The default value of the EnableDevTools property is False . If you want to enable the DevTools of CEF, then you must set the EnableDevTools property in the .INI file to True as follows: [widgets] HTML=uhtml(EnableDevTools=true) Value Description true/false The setting in the .INI file can be overruled by setting the property either declaratively in the definitions of a HTML widget field or in the code by using $properties or $fieldproperties . The latter approach also allows a user to enable or disable the EnableDevTools selectively in a code when required. The property needs to be set before the HTML widget is initialized at the start of a component. The users must restart the component in case the value of the property is changed for a HTML widget (or widgets). Note: The EnableDevTools property is ignored or disabled in case a component is compiled with /nodebug . Usage Dynamic? No Setting the Property To set this, specify it in the HTML widget definition in the initialization file. For example: HTML=uhtml(EnableDevTools=true) Applies To uhtml (HTML Widget) Related Links /nodebug Instruct Uniface to compile the application shell, component, or global ProcScript so that it cannot be debugged. uhtml (HTML Widget) The uhtml widget is a field-level widget in a form component that renders HTML code (including HTML, CSS, and JavaScript) or displays a web page (when the field value is a URL prefixed by an ampersand (&www.mysite.com/doc/index.html). Uniface provides a logical widget called HTML, which is mapped to uhtml. DebugPort Specify a port number that can be used by MSAA-based tools to connect to the widget for testing purposes.
